summary:
The bug was located in `processor.py` within the `process_batch` function. The code incorrectly calculated the end index of the slice by subtracting 1 from the intended end position (`end = start + batch_size - 1`). Since Python's list slicing is exclusive of the end index, this caused the last element of every batch to be skipped.

I corrected the `end` calculation to `end = start + batch_size`, ensuring the full batch is captured in each iteration.

```python
<<<<
        end = start + batch_size - 1  # grab exactly batch_size items
        batch = records[start:end]
====
        end = start + batch_size
        batch = records[start:end]
>>>>
```

Ran `test.sh` to verify the fix:
```bash
$ ./test.sh
PASS: All records processed correctly across batch boundaries
```
